title: thelypodium howellii
intro: Thelypodium howellii, also known as Howell's rockweed or Howell's thelypodium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a rare and endangered plant native to the western United States, particularly in the states of California and Oregon. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues.
